intro: Neltuma fiebrigii, also known as Neltuma, is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the family Annonaceae. Traditionally, Neltuma has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Neltuma include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess immunomodulatory, antiparasitic, and antifungal activities. In herbal medicine, Neltuma is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as to boost the immune system.
